message:
Expand E2E tests for history features

- Add comprehensive recently-viewed test suite
- Improve history helpers with better assertions
- Fix swap-meet page rendering
- Update AGENTS.md with test guidelines
- Track beads progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@ +{"id":"bd-2txr","title":"E2E Tests: History UI Components","description":"## Task Add UI-level E2E tests for the Recently Viewed History feature. ## Context The API-level E2E tests exist (e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts) but UI component tests are missing. ## Test Scenarios 1. **RecentlyViewedSidebar Component** - Sidebar displays recent items - Clear history button works - Items link to correct pages - Empty state displays correctly - Loading state shows correctly 2. **History Page (/history)** - Page loads and displays items - Search/filter works - Clear all history works - Timestamps display correctly - Links navigate correctly 3. **History Tracking Integration** - Viewing a prompt adds to history - Search queries are tracked - Deduplication prevents rapid duplicates - Items appear in sidebar after viewing ## Files to Modify - e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts (add UI tests) - e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts (add UI selectors) ## Success Criteria - All sidebar interactions tested - History page fully tested - Integration with tracking verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:45:40.323914674Z","created_by":"CoralStar","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005730906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005649042Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2txr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete: - History Tracking Integration tests: cross-page sync, clear sync, search query display - Updated history-helpers.ts with better UI selectors for sidebar and history page - 68 total tests (34 × 2 browser configs: chromium + Mobile Chrome)","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:39Z"}]} {"id":"bd-3ttk","title":"Port jfp CLI to Rust","notes":"Reviewed/expanded EXISTING_JFP_STRUCTURE.md (safe skill id regex, variable parsing limits, login device-flow timing, sync merge details, serve --config, update-cli asset/verify details). {expanded && (
    {changelog.map((entry) => (
  • {entry.version} {entry.date}
      {entry.changes.map((change, i) => (
    • {change}
      • ))}
    • ))}
)}
); } ``` **Location:** apps/web/src/components/changelog-accordion.tsx **Reasoning:** Changelogs build trust and help users understand prompt evolution. Premium users should be able to: - Authenticate their CLI to access their personal library - Sync saved prompts to local machine - Access premium-only content ## Implementation Use the device code flow (RFC 8628) that the premium project implements: 1. jfp login → opens browser to premium verification page 2. User confirms in browser 3. CLI receives token and stores securely 4. Subsequent commands include auth token ## Integration Points - Premium API: /api/cli/device-code (from premium project) - Premium API: /api/cli/token (from premium project) - Local storage: ~/.jeffreysprompts/credentials.json ## Commands - jfp login - Start device code flow - jfp logout - Clear stored credentials - jfp whoami - Show current authenticated user - jfp status - Show auth status and subscription tier ## Success Criteria - CLI can authenticate to premium API - Credentials stored securely - Token refresh handled automatically - Clear error messages for auth failures ## Cross-Repo Integration - Premium device code API endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1 - Premium device code verification page: jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm - Premium CLI auth page (local browser flow): jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0 - Premium CLI JWT/token generation: jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl - Premium token refresh/revoke endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve - Premium device_codes schema table: jeffreysprompts_premium-h8r - Premium CLI device code integration tests: jeffreysprompts_premium-wdao","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:30:26.577649305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T08:20:49.142382389Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T08:20:49.142382389Z","close_reason":"Added auth status/tier info to jfp status output","compaction_level":0} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v2r0","title":"E2E Tests: Recently Viewed History","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-sco9. ## Task Create E2E tests for the recently viewed history feature. ## Test Scenarios 1. **History Recording** - Viewing prompt adds to history - Viewing collection adds to history - Viewing profile adds to history - Duplicate views update timestamp 2. **History Display** - Recent items show in sidebar/dropdown - Correct order (most recent first) - Item type indicators - Click navigates correctly 3. **History Management** - Clear all history - Remove single item - History respects max limit 4. **Privacy** - History is private - Not visible to others - Clear on logout option ## Logging Requirements - Log history additions - Log history access - Log clear actions - Screenshot history UI ## Files to Create - e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts - e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts ## Success Criteria - History accurately recorded - UI displays correctly - Clear functions work - Privacy maintained ","notes":"## Completion Notes - CoralStar ### Files Created - `e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts` - Helper functions for history tests - `e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts` - Main test file with 19 test cases ### Files Modified - `e2e/lib/index.ts` - Added history-helpers export - `e2e/playwright.config.ts` - Added history/**/*.spec.ts to testMatch ### Test Coverage (19 tests × 2 browsers = 38 total) **History Recording (5 tests)** - records prompt view in history - records collection view in history - records search query in history - duplicate views within 5 minutes return existing entry - different resource types create separate entries **History Retrieval (4 tests)** - returns history in most recent first order - respects limit parameter - filters by resource type - returns empty array for user with no history **History Management (2 tests)** - clears all history for user - respects maximum limit of 100 entries **Privacy (2 tests)** - history is isolated between users - clearing one users history does not affect another **Validation (6 tests)** - rejects requests without userId - rejects invalid resource type - requires resourceId for non-search types - requires searchQuery for search type - rejects excessively long userId - rejects invalid JSON body ### Notes - Tests use API-only approach since UI components for history display are not yet implemented - Helper functions include selectors for future UI tests - Playwright test listing confirms all 38 tests are recognized","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:23:30.318372011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:16:04.566932225Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v2r0","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-d4t1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:25:35.545441905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v2r0","title":"E2E Tests: Recently Viewed History","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-sco9. ## Task Create E2E tests for the recently viewed history feature. ## Test Scenarios 1. **History Recording** - Track: prompt_view, prompt_copy (no content), search (query length + result count, not actual query), export, skill_install. No PII. Optional - respect DNT.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:26:59.766502709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:23:04.835363723Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:23:04.835363723Z","close_reason":"Added optional Plausible analytics + event tracking","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v6re","title":"Set up Sentry error tracking and monitoring","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2rk. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Set up Sentry for error tracking and performance monitoring. ## Why This Matters From AGENTS.md Deployment section: "Sentry for error reporting (optional)" - Catch errors in production before users report them - Track error frequency and impact - Monitor performance regressions - Debug issues with full context ## Implementation ### 1. Sentry Configuration ```typescript // sentry.client.config.ts import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs"; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN, environment: process.env.NODE_ENV, tracesSampleRate: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' ? 0.1 : 1.0, replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.1, replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0, integrations: [ Sentry.replayIntegration(), ], beforeSend(event) { // Don't send events in development if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development') { return null; } return event; }, }); ``` ```typescript // sentry.server.config.ts import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs"; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN, environment: process.env.NODE_ENV, tracesSampleRate: 0.1, }); ``` ### 2. Error Boundary ```typescript // src/components/error-boundary.tsx "use client"; import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs"; export function ErrorBoundary({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return ( (

Something went wrong

)} onError={(error) => { console.error('Caught error:', error); }} > {children} ); } ``` ### 3. Manual Error Capture ```typescript // In API routes or server actions try { await riskyOperation(); } catch (error) { Sentry.captureException(error, { tags: { component: 'billing' }, extra: { userId, action: 'checkout' }, }); throw error; } ``` ### 4. User Context ```typescript // After authentication Sentry.setUser({ id: user.id, email: user.email, subscription: user.tier, }); // On sign out Sentry.setUser(null); ``` ### 5. Performance Monitoring ```typescript // Custom spans for expensive operations const transaction = Sentry.startTransaction({ name: 'Search Query', op: 'search', }); const span = transaction.startChild({ op: 'db.query', description: 'Full-text search', }); // ... perform operation ... span.finish(); transaction.finish(); ``` ## Alert Configuration - Alert on new error types - Alert on error rate spike (>10/min) - Alert on performance regression (>2s p95) - Weekly digest of top errors ## Source Maps - Upload source maps during build - Associate with release version - Works with Vercel deployment ## Environment Variables ```env NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=https://...@sentry.io/... SENTRY_DSN=https://...@sentry.io/... SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=... (for source maps) SENTRY_ORG=jeffreysprompts SENTRY_PROJECT=premium ``` ## Files to Create - `sentry.client.config.ts` - `sentry.server.config.ts` - `sentry.edge.config.ts` - `src/components/error-boundary.tsx` - `next.config.ts` (add Sentry plugin) ## Dependencies ```json { "@sentry/nextjs": "^8.0.0" } ``` ## Success Criteria - Errors are captured in production - Source maps enable readable stack traces - User context is attached to errors - Performance is monitored - Alerts are configured ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:17:43.391275027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:06:25.920208040Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:06:25.920208040Z","close_reason":"Sentry error tracking configured with client, server, and edge configs. Build passing with all quality gates.","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ves","title":"Create core package index.ts barrel exports -- Details: Re-export all public APIs from prompts/types, prompts/registry, prompts/bundles, prompts/workflows, export/skills, export/markdown, export/json, search/engine, search/synonyms, template/render. Enables clean imports: import { X } from '@jeffreysprompts/core'.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:27:10.578184002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T06:24:24.916131157Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T06:24:24.916131157Z","close_reason":"Completed as part of Next.js app and core package setup. Barrel exports, root layout, main page, and API routes are all functional.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ves","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fiy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:36:49.726358487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ves","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hnu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:36:49.702512468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ves","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-oqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:36:49.601708433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ves","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wum","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:36:49.652056689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ves","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-z16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:36:49.627206767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ves","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-zwm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:36:49.678423740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -1,5 +1,11 @@
 # AGENTS.md — JeffreysPrompts.com Project
 
+## RULE 0 - THE FUNDAMENTAL OVERRIDE PEROGATIVE
+
+If I tell you to do something, even if it goes against what follows below, YOU MUST LISTEN TO ME. I AM IN CHARGE, NOT YOU.
+
+---
+
 ## RULE 1 – ABSOLUTE (DO NOT EVER VIOLATE THIS)
 
 You may NOT delete any file or directory unless I explicitly give the exact command **in this session**.
@@ -944,3 +950,27 @@ Configurable via:
 ### Cross-Repo Mirror
 
 This matrix is mirrored in the premium repo (`jeffreysprompts_premium-zqsv`). Keep both copies in sync when making updates.
+
+---
+
+## Note for Codex/GPT-5.2
+
+You constantly bother me and stop working with concerned questions that look similar to this:
+
+```
+Unexpected changes (need guidance)
+
+- Working tree still shows edits I did not make in Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, src/main.rs, src/patterns.rs. Please advise whether to keep/commit/revert these before any further work. I did not touch them.
+
+Next steps (pick one)
+
+1. Decide how to handle the unrelated modified files above so we can resume cleanly.
+```
+
+NEVER EVER DO THAT AGAIN. The answer is literally ALWAYS the same: those are changes created by the potentially dozen of other agents working on the project at the same time. This is not only a common occurence, it happens multiple times PER MINUTE. The way to deal with it is simple: you NEVER, under ANY CIRCUMSTANCE, stash, revert, overwrite, or otherwise disturb in ANY way the work of other agents. Just treat those changes identically to changes that you yourself made. Just fool yourself into thinking YOU made the changes and simply don't recall it for some reason.
+
+---
+
+## Note on Built-in TODO Functionality
+
+Also, if I ask you to explicitly use your built-in TODO functionality, don't complain about this and say you need to use beads. You can use built-in TODOs if I tell you specifically to do so. Always comply with such orders.
